title: dryopteris gongylodes var. hirsuta
intro: Dryopteris gongylodes var. hirsuta, also known as the hirsute male fern, is a variety of fern that belongs to the Dryopteris genus and is native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The hirsute male fern has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
